clusters.title: Concurrent URMA window rollover can drop or misclassify samples

member_findings.title: Concurrent window rollover can drop and misclassify URMA samples
member_findings.impact: When one request wins the windowStartTimeMs_ compare-exchange it publishes the new window start before exchanging the counters. A concurrent request can then observe the new start, record into successCount_ or failureCount_ for the new window, and have that sample removed by the winner's exchange at line 57. Under concurrent client traffic this corrupts both the settled window and the next window, which can suppress a needed failover or trigger one from samples that did not belong to the expired window.
member_findings.fix: Make window rollover and counter ownership atomic as one invariant: protect start/counters with a mutex, or use a generation/double-buffer design where writers increment counters for a specific observed window generation and the settling thread only swaps out that generation after preventing further writes to it.
